title: Wire non-duplicate sync into runtime workflow
goal: Wire SyncedSettings sync store into runtime: startup hydration, dual-write on mark/unmark, sync listener registration, end-to-end integration tests, and documentation.
in_scope: Startup hydration: read SyncedSettings, diff with local DB, bidirectional sync
Inject NonDuplicateSyncStore into mark/unmark flow and notify handlers
Register sync listener at startup (wire M012 registerSyncListener)
Bidirectional first-sync bootstrap (local pairs -> SyncedSettings on first upgrade)
Per-library hydration for all non-feed libraries
End-to-end integration test: mark -> local DB + SyncedSettings -> simulate sync -> hydrate
Documentation: CLAUDE.md open risks, code comments
out_of_scope: Performance optimization for very large pair sets (deferred to backlog)
acceptance: On startup, existing local non-duplicate pairs are written to SyncedSettings (first-upgrade bootstrap)
On startup, SyncedSettings pairs not in local DB are imported (sync-from-remote hydration)
toggleNonDuplicates mark writes to both local DB and SyncedSettings
toggleNonDuplicates unmark removes from both local DB and SyncedSettings
Deleting an item cleans up both local DB and SyncedSettings
onSyncDownload listener is registered at startup and properly wired
Hydration runs for all non-feed libraries
End-to-end test passes: mark locally, read SyncedSettings, verify pair exists
SyncedSettings write failure does not block local mark/unmark
MANUAL: mark non-duplicate in Zotero 9 -> trigger sync -> verify setting in sync log
